title: Conditionals
beginnerExplanation: Conditionals allow your code to make decisions, executing different blocks of code based on whether a condition is true or false.
intermediateExplanation: Games need logic branches. A door only opens IF you have the key. Without conditionals, a program would just do the exact same thing every time.

realWorldAnalogy.title: A Fork in the Road
realWorldAnalogy.description: Imagine reaching a crossroad with a sign: "IF you have a sword, go left. ELSE, go right." Conditionals are how Python chooses which path to take.

mentalModel: Imagine reaching a crossroad with a sign: "IF you have a sword, go left. ELSE, go right." Conditionals are how Python chooses which path to take.

commonMistakes: Forgetting the colon at the end of the if statement.

debuggingMission.scenario: An adventurer is trying to use Conditionals but hit a common pitfall: Forgetting the colon at the end of the if statement.
debuggingMission.explanation: Make sure to follow the best practices: Keep conditions simple and readable.

practiceQuestions.question: Which keyword is used to check an additional condition if the first one fails?
practiceQuestions.explanation: Python uses "elif", which is short for "else if".

challengeQuestions.question: What is the correct keyword or operator most commonly associated with Conditionals?
hints: Look at the syntax summary of the topic.
Check standard keyword usage in Python.

projectExercise.description: Implement a clean snippet demonstrating the use of Conditionals.
